title: あなたはSecurity Analystです。OWASP Top 10を参考に分析します。 ## チェック項目 - SQLインジェクション - XSS - CSRF - 認証・認可の不備 - 機密情報のハードコード - コマンドインジェクション ## 評価（3点満点） 3点: 問題なし 2点: 軽微な懸念 1点: 要対応 0点: 重大な脆弱性 ## 出力形式（JSON） { "score": 0-3, "vulnerabilities": [{ "severity": "critical|high|medium|low", "type": "", "description": "", "rem...
summary_text: { "score": 1, "vulnerabilities": [ { "severity": "high", "type": "command_injection", "description": "/Users/masayuki/.claude/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/delegate-copilot.js:29-31 uses execSync with a shell command string built from user-controlled task/file content: `copilot -p ${JSON.stringify(prompt)} --allow-all-tools`. JSON.stringify does not prevent shell expansion inside double quotes, so `$(...)`, backticks, or similar metacharacters in the prompt can execute arbitrary commands if this wrapper is invoked.", "remediation": "Replace execSync(string) with spawn/execFile and pass arguments as an array, or pass the prompt via stdin. Do not invoke a shell. Add a regression test with payloads containing `$()` and backticks." }, { "severity": "medium", "type": "secret_management", "description": "/Users/masayuki/.claude/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-worker/.env exists with runtime credentials and permissions `-rw-r--r--` (0644). The file contains active connection material such as SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY. Assumption split: if this is only an anon key, impact is limited to public client scope plus environment disclosure; if a service-role key is ever placed here, exposure becomes high because the same code path accepts SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY.", "remediation": "Restrict to 0600 immediately, move secrets to a dedicated secret store or launchd environment file with tight permissions, keep `.env` out of version control, and rotate the key if multi-user host exposure cannot be ruled out." }, { "severity": "low", "type": "unsafe_env_loading", "description": "/Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/run-poller.sh:11-13, /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/run-scheduler.sh:10-12, and /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/run-gha24.sh:11-13 use `eval \"$(grep ... ~/.zshrc)\"` to import API keys. This creates a shell-evaluation path from dotfile contents into scheduled execution. Assumption: exploitability requires prior modification of `~/.zshrc`, so this is not an initial-entry vulnerability, but it increases blast radius and persistence if the shell profile is tampered with.", "remediation": "Stop using `eval` on grep output. Source a dedicated env file with fixed ownership/permissions, or parse key-value pairs without shell evaluation." } ], "summary": "Static scan of ~/.claude/fallback/, ~/.claude/auto-task/, and ~/.claude/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/ found no confirmed hardcoded live secrets in source code, no world-writable/setuid/setgid files, and no direct evidence of SQL injection/XSS/CSRF in the scanned CLI/worker surface. The main actionable risks are one high-severity command-injection flaw in the Copilot wrapper and one medium secret-handling weakness from a world-readable runtime .env file. Dependency verification was limited: ~/.claude/fallback/ had no standard dependency manifest, and `npm audit` for auto-task and fugue-worker could not reach the npm advisory service on 2026-03-28, so no online CVE confirmation was possible from this environment." }
